items.title: VCs: please take this low valuation since I’m investing in you and my conviction is worth the p…
items.content: VCs: please take this low valuation since I’m investing in you and my conviction is worth the price. I’m invested for the long long term. 6 months later.. Same VC: take the highest possible valuation because why not. Who cares about the partner or fund name. Less dilution is good. More money is good. (Also it’ll help me show marks to LPs so take it bro please). Try to avoid these VCs like the plague. All they care about is their “deals” getting markups. I get it - generating returns is the job.…

items.title: I was writing a (relatively) long essay on all my learnings of transitioning to a VC ~two years…
items.content: I was writing a (relatively) long essay on all my learnings of transitioning to a VC ~two years ago.. But, decided to whittle it down to a tight 11 bullet point list (some are cliches, but oh so true): 1. Get on the flight -don’t question it. 2. Deals often die on the weekends. 3. If it’s not wired, it’s not done yet. 4. Conviction is never found in the data room. 5. Naïvety is a virtue, not a weakness. 6. In early stage, pick two: founder, market, product -then believe that the third catches u…

items.title: Sometimes VCs will ask questions just to test your depth (market, technical, your obsession). T…
items.content: Sometimes VCs will ask questions just to test your depth (market, technical, your obsession). The best founders (imo) are able to argue both sides of an argument with extreme clarity. There’s rarely something VCs ask that should make you *really* think about your business. If they do it consistently, try to get them on the cap table!

items.title: Inspired by @karpathy & @FarzaTV, introducing LLMwiki.. fully open source to help build yours.…
items.content: Inspired by @karpathy & @FarzaTV, introducing LLMwiki.. fully open source to help build yours. Inputs were tweets, bookmarks, iMessage/WhatsApp, and all my writing. Spent a bunch of time refining the frontend design to make it look great. Even though every single article here was written by AI, it was able to make surprisingly sharp connections. To make yours, just give the repo to Claude Code and it'll guide you!

items.title: At some point, early stage founders decided to optimize for views and funding instead of focusi…
items.content: At some point, early stage founders decided to optimize for views and funding instead of focusing on product and retention.. And, it’s starting to show. One of the very first things I do is look at change logs (or feature releases) of supposedly flashy companies. Or read case studies if they have any. And for weeks, I’ll barely see any changes. And, these are companies that would benefit from sharing this info to use for marketing. Instead of spending your time optimizing the viral launch video…
